title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 1 (Book 3)
source: CPE Practice Tests 3, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Sharing a Room

Before starting university, I hadn't given more than a passing thought to what could go wrong sharing a room with another student. I had simply assumed that my roommate-to-be would be rather like me — at university to have fun but mainly to learn. Within a couple of weeks, I was (1) ______ aware of how wrong I was. The (2) ______ proved to be the complete opposite of the studious type I had envisaged. It didn't take long before things came to a (3) ______ and we had our first major row about, of all things, the alarm clock going off every day at 7:00 am. The next day, I (4) ______ up the courage to have a quiet word with our student liaison officer, who explained that sharing a room with someone is about learning to compromise. I had to (5) ______ up the pros and cons and try to come to some sort of arrangement. Compromise soon proved to be the key as my roommate's initial hostility was slowly (6) ______ down once I managed to persuade him to agree to a workable set of ground rules. From then on it was relatively smooth sailing, though I still had to keep a (7) ______ grip on my temper. Two semesters later, we (8) ______ up the best of friends.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'all too aware' là collocation cố định nghĩa là 'nhận ra một cách đau đớn/rõ ràng quá mức'. 'All too' + tính từ diễn tả mức độ quá mức không mong muốn. Bẫy: 'too much' (B) không đi với tính từ 'aware' — 'too much' dùng với danh từ hoặc đứng sau động từ. 'more so' (A) cần ngữ cảnh so sánh trước đó. 'as well' (C) nghĩa là 'cũng vậy', không phù hợp ngữ cảnh. Chuyên đề: Collocations với 'too'.
questions.answerLocation: all too aware of how wrong I was
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'The latter' dùng để chỉ người/vật thứ hai trong hai đối tượng đã đề cập trước đó (tác giả vs. roommate). Câu trước nói 'my roommate-to-be would be rather like me', tạo ra hai đối tượng so sánh, và 'the latter' chỉ roommate. Bẫy: 'second' (A) không dùng với mạo từ 'the' theo cách này để chỉ người thứ hai vừa đề cập. 'later' (B) là trạng từ chỉ thời gian, không phải determiner. 'other' (D) cần 'the other' và không phù hợp ngữ pháp ở đây. Chuyên đề: Determiners & Reference words.
questions.answerLocation: The latter proved to be the complete opposite
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'come to a head' là idiom cố định nghĩa là 'đến điểm bùng phát/đỉnh điểm'. Khi một tình huống 'comes to a head', nó đạt đến mức không thể bỏ qua và phải giải quyết. Phù hợp với ngữ cảnh xung đột leo thang dẫn đến 'first major row'. Bẫy: 'moment' (B) không tạo thành idiom 'come to a moment'. 'count' (C) không phù hợp — 'come to a count' không tồn tại. 'strike' (D) cũng không tạo idiom phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Idioms — come to a head.
questions.answerLocation: things came to a head and we had our first major row
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'plucked up the courage' là collocation cố định nghĩa là 'lấy hết can đảm'. 'Pluck up courage/the courage to do something' = tự thúc đẩy bản thân vượt qua sợ hãi. Bẫy: 'built up' (B) có thể dùng với 'courage' nhưng không tự nhiên bằng 'plucked up'. 'drawn up' (C) nghĩa là 'soạn thảo' (ví dụ: draw up a plan), không đi với 'courage'. 'spent up' (D) không phải phrasal verb chuẩn. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — pluck up.
questions.answerLocation: plucked up the courage to have a quiet word
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'weigh up the pros and cons' là collocation chuẩn nghĩa là 'cân nhắc ưu và nhược điểm'. 'Weigh up' = xem xét cẩn thận các khía cạnh tích cực và tiêu cực trước khi quyết định. Bẫy: 'deliberate' (A) nghĩa là 'cân nhắc' nhưng không đi với 'up' — nó là intransitive verb hoặc adjective. 'measure' (B) không tạo thành phrasal verb 'measure up the pros and cons'. 'dissect' (C) nghĩa là 'mổ xẻ/phân tích' nhưng không đi với 'up'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — weigh up.
questions.answerLocation: weigh up the pros and cons
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'breaking down' = giảm dần, xóa bỏ (rào cản). 'Hostility was slowly breaking down' = sự thù địch dần tan biến khi đạt được thỏa thuận. 'Break down barriers/hostility' là collocation phổ biến. Bẫy: 'wearing down' (A) cũng có nghĩa tương tự ('mài mòn') nhưng thường dùng với chủ ngữ là người ('he wore down her resistance'), không phù hợp với cấu trúc bị động ở đây. 'fading down' (B) không phải phrasal verb chuẩn — chỉ có 'fading away'. 'beating down' (D) nghĩa là 'đánh bại/mặc cả giảm giá', không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — break down.
questions.answerLocation: hostility was slowly breaking down
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'keep a tight grip on' = kiểm soát chặt chẽ. Đây là collocation cố định, đặc biệt khi nói về cảm xúc: 'keep a tight grip on one's temper/emotions'. Bẫy: 'strong grip' (A) thường dùng cho sức mạnh vật lý (nắm chặt tay). 'firm grip' (B) cũng có thể dùng được nhưng với 'temper' (tính khí), 'tight' là lựa chọn chuẩn và tự nhiên nhất ở cấp độ CPE. 'secure grip' (D) nghĩa là 'nắm chắc' theo nghĩa đen, không dùng với trừu tượng như 'temper'. Chuyên đề: Collocations với 'grip'.
questions.answerLocation: keep a tight grip on my temper
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'wound up the best of friends' — 'wind up' (quá khứ: wound up) = kết thúc ở trạng thái nào đó, tương tự 'end up'. 'We wound up the best of friends' = cuối cùng chúng tôi trở thành bạn thân. Bẫy: 'ended up' (A) cũng có nghĩa tương tự và rất phổ biến, nhưng ở cấp độ CPE, 'wound up' thể hiện vốn từ vựng phong phú hơn. 'turned up' (B) nghĩa là 'xuất hiện'. 'made up' (D) nghĩa là 'làm hòa' hoặc 'bịa đặt', không đi với 'the best of friends' theo cấu trúc này. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — wind up.
questions.answerLocation: we wound up the best of friends
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Fake Rolex Watches

Rolex is ONE of the most sought (9) ______ brand names in the timepiece industry. (10) ______ from the design and movement of a Rolex to its price screams quality. Thanks to the outrageous prestige of the Rolex brand, Rolex watches are (11) ______ the most counterfeited watches in the world. The Rolex brand carries (12) ______ it a sense of status and luxury that is recognised worldwide. It (13) ______ without saying that thrift motivates informed fake Rolex purchases. Most people simply don't have the money to purchase the genuine article. There is only one way to tell a high-quality Rolex knock-off (14) ______ the real deal and that is to get an expert to open up the watch. At this point, most experts, most of the time, can spot a fake by looking at the watch's movement. But not always. Sometimes, even experts can have a hard time telling the difference, especially (15) ______ they are looking at a high-quality fake. And Rolex replicas are only going to get better. This means they are going to become (16) ______ harder to detect.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (9): one of the most sought ______ brand names
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AFTER. 'sought after' = được săn đón, ưa chuộng. Đây là phrasal adjective rất phổ biến trong tiếng Anh. 'One of the most sought-after brand names' = một trong những thương hiệu được săn đón nhất. Lưu ý: 'sought after' khi đứng trước danh từ thường viết có gạch nối (sought-after), nhưng trong bài điền từ chỉ cần điền 'after'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal adjectives. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: most sought after brand names
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (10): ______ from the design and movement of a Rolex to its price screams quality.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EVERYTHING. 'Everything from X to Y' = mọi thứ từ X đến Y. Đây là cấu trúc cố định dùng để liệt kê phạm vi rộng. 'Everything from the design... to its price screams quality' = mọi thứ từ thiết kế đến giá cả đều toát lên chất lượng. Bẫy thường gặp: 'Anything' cũng có thể đi với 'from...to' nhưng không phù hợp vì câu nói về tổng thể (screams quality), không phải khả năng. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: Everything from the design and movement
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (11): Rolex watches are ______ the most counterfeited watches in the world.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AMONG (hoặc AMONGST). 'among the most + superlative' = nằm trong số những... nhất. 'Rolex watches are among the most counterfeited' = đồng hồ Rolex nằm trong số bị làm giả nhiều nhất. Bẫy: 'between' chỉ dùng cho 2 đối tượng cụ thể, không dùng với superlative theo cách này. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — among vs. between.
questions.answerLocation: are among the most counterfeited
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (12): The Rolex brand carries ______ it a sense of status and luxury.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITH. 'carry with it' = mang theo, đi kèm với. 'The brand carries with it a sense of status' = thương hiệu mang theo cảm giác về địa vị. Đây là cấu trúc 'carry something with one/it' rất phổ biến trong văn viết trang trọng. Bẫy: 'in' hoặc 'on' không phù hợp ngữ pháp. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — carry with.
questions.answerLocation: carries with it a sense of status
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (13): It ______ without saying that thrift motivates informed fake Rolex purchases.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: GOES. 'It goes without saying' = không cần phải nói (ai cũng biết). Đây là idiom cố định rất phổ biến trong tiếng Anh. 'It goes without saying that...' dùng để giới thiệu một điều hiển nhiên. Bẫy: không có từ nào khác phù hợp ngữ pháp và ngữ nghĩa. Chuyên đề: Idioms — go without saying.
questions.answerLocation: It goes without saying
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (14): to tell a high-quality Rolex knock-off ______ the real deal
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FROM. 'tell X from Y' = phân biệt X với Y. 'Tell a knock-off from the real deal' = phân biệt hàng nhái với hàng thật. Bẫy: 'apart' cũng dùng được nhưng cần cấu trúc khác: 'tell X and Y apart'. Với cấu trúc 'tell X ___ Y', chỉ có 'from' phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — tell from.
questions.answerLocation: tell a high-quality Rolex knock-off from the real deal
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (15): even experts can have a hard time telling the difference, especially ______ they are looking at a high-quality fake.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IF (hoặc WHEN). 'especially if/when' = đặc biệt là nếu/khi. Câu nói rằng ngay cả chuyên gia cũng khó phân biệt, đặc biệt nếu/khi họ đang xem xét hàng giả chất lượng cao. 'If' nhấn mạnh điều kiện, 'when' nhấn mạnh tình huống xảy ra. Cả hai đều chấp nhận được. Chuyên đề: Conjunctions — conditional/temporal clauses.
questions.answerLocation: especially if they are looking at a high-quality fake
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (16): they are going to become ______ harder to detect.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EVEN. 'even + comparative' = còn... hơn nữa. 'Even harder to detect' = còn khó phát hiện hơn nữa. 'Even' ở đây là adverb of degree dùng để nhấn mạnh so sánh hơn. Bẫy: 'much' hoặc 'far' cũng có thể dùng với comparative nhưng 'even' là lựa chọn tự nhiên nhất trong ngữ cảnh này (nhấn mạnh xu hướng tăng dần). Chuyên đề: Adverbs of degree với comparative.
questions.answerLocation: become even harder to detect
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Employee Sick Leave

The European Court of Justice has ruled that workers who become ill during their holidays have the right to take ADDITIONAL paid time off later. The Luxembourg-based court stated that 'the purpose of (17) ______ (TITLE) to paid annual leave is to enable the worker to rest and enjoy a period of (18) ______ (RELAX) and leisure'. It said this right applies even if a worker becomes unwell while on leave, even if it is due to a pre-existing (19) ______ (AIL). Business groups in the UK say that the courts' rules are (20) ______ (CONTRADICT). Business groups in the UK warn that applying the courts' rules will be (21) ______ (DETRIMENT) to the wider UK economy and say that the rulings (22) ______ (LINE) the need for Britain's ministers to resist the court. But, unfortunately, while the UK has an opt-out from the EU's working time (23) ______ (DIRECT), it does not extend to annual leave. The UK government estimates that adopting the rules will cost British employers more than £100 million annually. The UK's Business Secretary described the court's rulings as 'an unnecessary burden on businesses' and 'completely (24) ______ (JUSTIFY) during a time of economic strain'.

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct word form.

questions.instruction: Gap (17): the purpose of ______ (TITLE) to paid annual leave
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ENTITLEMENT (từ gốc: TITLE). Thêm tiền tố 'en-' và hậu tố '-ment' để tạo danh từ 'entitlement' = quyền được hưởng. 'Entitlement to paid annual leave' = quyền nghỉ phép có lương. Cần danh từ vì đứng sau giới từ 'of'. Bẫy: 'ENTITLED' (tính từ) không phù hợp vì cần danh từ sau 'of'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (en- + root + -ment).
questions.answerLocation: entitlement to paid annual leave
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (18): enjoy a period of ______ (RELAX) and leisure
questions.explanation: Đáp án: RELAXATION (từ gốc: RELAX). Thêm hậu tố '-ation' để tạo danh từ. 'A period of relaxation' = một khoảng thời gian thư giãn. Cần danh từ vì đứng sau giới từ 'of' và song song với danh từ 'leisure'. Bẫy: 'RELAXING' (tính từ/gerund) có thể gây nhầm nhưng không phù hợp cấu trúc song song 'relaxation and leisure'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ation).
questions.answerLocation: a period of relaxation and leisure
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (19): even if it is due to a pre-existing ______ (AIL)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AILMENT (từ gốc: AIL). Thêm hậu tố '-ment' để tạo danh từ. 'A pre-existing ailment' = bệnh có sẵn từ trước. Cần danh từ đếm được vì đứng sau mạo từ 'a'. Bẫy: 'AILING' (tính từ = đang ốm) không phù hợp vì cần danh từ sau 'pre-existing' + mạo từ 'a'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ment).
questions.answerLocation: a pre-existing ailment
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (20): the courts' rules are ______ (CONTRADICT)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONTRADICTORY (từ gốc: CONTRADICT). Thêm hậu tố '-ory' để tạo tính từ. 'The rules are contradictory' = các quy tắc mâu thuẫn/tự mâu thuẫn. Cần tính từ vì đứng sau động từ liên kết 'are'. Bẫy: 'CONTRADICTION' (danh từ) không phù hợp vì cần tính từ làm bổ ngữ cho 'are'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-ory).
questions.answerLocation: the courts' rules are contradictory
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (21): applying the courts' rules will be ______ (DETRIMENT) to the wider UK economy
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DETRIMENTAL (từ gốc: DETRIMENT). Thêm hậu tố '-al' để tạo tính từ. 'Detrimental to the economy' = gây hại cho nền kinh tế. Cần tính từ sau 'will be'. Collocation chuẩn: 'detrimental to' + danh từ. Bẫy: 'DETRIMENTALLY' (trạng từ) không phù hợp vì cần tính từ bổ ngữ cho 'will be'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-al).
questions.answerLocation: will be detrimental to the wider UK economy
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (22): the rulings ______ (LINE) the need for Britain's ministers to resist
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNDERLINE (từ gốc: LINE). Thêm tiền tố 'under-' để tạo động từ. 'The rulings underline the need' = các phán quyết nhấn mạnh/gạch dưới sự cần thiết. Cần động từ chia thì hiện tại đơn (chủ ngữ 'the rulings' số nhiều). Bẫy: 'OUTLINE' (phác thảo) cũng có thể nhưng 'underline' mạnh mẽ hơn về nghĩa 'nhấn mạnh'. 'LINING' không phù hợp ngữ pháp. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Verbs (under- prefix).
questions.answerLocation: the rulings underline the need
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (23): the EU's working time ______ (DIRECT)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DIRECTIVE (từ gốc: DIRECT). Thêm hậu tố '-ive' để tạo danh từ. 'Working time directive' = chỉ thị về thời gian làm việc (thuật ngữ EU). Đây là thuật ngữ chính thức của EU: 'EU Working Time Directive'. Cần danh từ vì đứng sau tính từ 'working time'. Bẫy: 'DIRECTION' (hướng) không phù hợp ngữ nghĩa — đây là văn bản pháp luật, không phải hướng đi. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ive as noun).
questions.answerLocation: working time directive
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (24): completely ______ (JUSTIFY) during a time of economic strain
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNJUSTIFIABLE (từ gốc: JUSTIFY). Thêm tiền tố phủ định 'un-' và hậu tố '-able' rồi thay đổi dạng từ: justify → justifiable → unjustifiable. 'Completely unjustifiable' = hoàn toàn không thể biện minh/biện hộ. Cần tính từ phủ định vì ngữ cảnh phê phán ('unnecessary burden'). Bẫy QUAN TRỌNG: nhiều thí sinh quên thêm tiền tố phủ định 'un-' và chỉ viết 'justifiable' — nhưng ngữ cảnh yêu cầu nghĩa phủ định (Business Secretary đang chỉ trích). Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Negative adjectives (un- + root + -able).
questions.answerLocation: completely unjustifiable during a time of economic strain
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

25. I think you will find that a visit to the British Museum will be helpful. (WHILE)

I think it will ______ to visit the British Museum.

26. He fooled everyone with his relaxed and confident manner. (TAKEN)

Everyone ______ fact that he seemed so relaxed and confident.

27. I don't mind whether he comes on Friday or Saturday. (SAME)

It's ______ whether he comes on Friday or Saturday.

28. There's no need for me to tell you how important this deal is for the company. (SAYING)

It ______ is very important for the company.

29. Her lack of qualifications didn't deter her from applying for the job. (PUT)

She ______ by her lack of qualifications.

30. I don't think he will make a very good actor. (CUT)

In my opinion, ______ an actor.

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gap for each sentence below using 3 to 8 words.

questions.instruction: I think you will find that a visit to the British Museum will be helpful. (WHILE) -> I think it will ______ to visit the British Museum.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'be worth your while'. 'It will be worth your while to...' = sẽ đáng để bạn dành thời gian/công sức. Đây là cấu trúc cố định 'be worth one's while' — 'while' ở đây nghĩa là 'thời gian, công sức'. Bẫy: nhiều thí sinh viết 'be worthwhile' nhưng phải dùng key word 'WHILE' riêng lẻ (không được thay đổi từ cho sẵn). 'Worthwhile' là một từ ghép, không tách ra được 'while'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — worth one's while.
questions.answerLocation: be worth your while to visit
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: He fooled everyone with his relaxed and confident manner. (TAKEN) -> Everyone ______ fact that he seemed so relaxed and confident.
acceptedAnswers: had been taken in by the
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'was taken in by the'. 'be taken in by' = bị lừa bởi. 'Everyone was taken in by the fact that...' = Mọi người đều bị lừa bởi việc anh ta có vẻ thoải mái và tự tin. Phrasal verb 'take in' (lừa dối) ở thể bị động. Bẫy: 'taken aback' (ngạc nhiên) không phù hợp nghĩa 'fooled'. 'taken over' (tiếp quản) cũng không đúng. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — take in (passive voice). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: was taken in by the fact
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I don't mind whether he comes on Friday or Saturday. (SAME) -> It's ______ whether he comes on Friday or Saturday.
acceptedAnswers: all the same to me whether
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'all the same to me'. 'It's all the same to me' = với tôi thì như nhau, tôi không quan tâm. Đây là idiom cố định diễn tả sự không phân biệt/không quan tâm giữa các lựa chọn. Câu gốc 'I don't mind' = tôi không phiền → 'It's all the same to me' diễn đạt cùng ý. Bẫy: 'the same thing' không phù hợp cấu trúc ngữ pháp. Chuyên đề: Idioms — all the same.
questions.answerLocation: all the same to me whether
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: There's no need for me to tell you how important this deal is for the company. (SAYING) -> It ______ is very important for the company.
acceptedAnswers: goes without saying that this deal
questions.correctAnswer: goes without saying that this deal
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'goes without saying that this deal'. 'It goes without saying that...' = không cần phải nói rằng... (ai cũng biết). Câu gốc 'There's no need for me to tell you' ≈ 'It goes without saying'. Cần thêm 'that this deal' để nối với phần sau 'is very important for the company'. Bẫy: quên thêm 'that this deal' sẽ làm câu thiếu chủ ngữ. Chuyên đề: Idioms — go without saying.
questions.answerLocation: goes without saying that this deal is very important
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Her lack of qualifications didn't deter her from applying for the job. (PUT) -> She ______ by her lack of qualifications.
acceptedAnswers: was not put off from applying
questions.correctAnswer: was not put off from applying
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'was not put off from applying'. 'be put off' = bị nản lòng/ngăn cản. 'She was not put off from applying by her lack of qualifications' = Cô ấy không bị nản chí khi nộp đơn bởi thiếu bằng cấp. Câu gốc dùng 'deter from' → chuyển sang 'put off from' (bị động). Bẫy: 'put up with' (chịu đựng) hoàn toàn khác nghĩa. 'put off' cần 'from + V-ing' để diễn đạt 'ngăn cản làm gì'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — put off (passive). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: was not put off from applying by her lack
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I don't think he will make a very good actor. (CUT) -> In my opinion, ______ an actor.
acceptedAnswers: he is not cut out to be
he's not cut out to be
he isn't cut out to be
questions.correctAnswer: he is not cut out to be
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'he is not cut out to be'. 'be cut out for/to be' = có tố chất/phù hợp để làm gì. 'He is not cut out to be an actor' = Anh ta không có tố chất làm diễn viên. Câu gốc 'I don't think he will make a very good actor' = tôi không nghĩ anh ta sẽ làm diễn viên giỏi → 'he is not cut out to be an actor'. Bẫy: 'cut off' (cắt đứt) hoặc 'cut down' (giảm bớt) hoàn toàn khác nghĩa. Chuyên đề: Idioms — be cut out for/to be.
questions.answerLocation: he is not cut out to be an actor
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from a novel. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Emma's Visit

Emma Boylan drove at her usual breakneck speed through the city traffic, manoeuvring with consummate skill to get past the slower-moving vehicles. Her green TR7 attracted a lot of attention, most of it hostile. Apart from Vinny, few people had seen the car before she bought it. Vinny had inspected it and expressed qualms. Was it not a bit too powerful for a woman driver, he offered. That clinched it. She bought the car the next day.

Today she had decided to call on her father unannounced at his practice. Her father did not encourage the habit, nor was it something she particularly enjoyed herself. Twice a year she felt the need to present herself at his offices. It helped maintain an illusion of father-daughter togetherness that she felt was expected of them.

She was shown into his office by one of the junior solicitors, a young man so thin and pale that he looked as though a breath of fresh air might blow him away. Her father's office, lined with heavy bookshelves and hung with photographs of himself and his family at various stages of their lives, struck her as oppressive. She could not envisage working in such an environment. Emma suspected that the display was more for the benefit of visitors than for the man sitting behind the desk.

Her father, a tall broad-shouldered man whose face bore traces of what must once have been considerable good looks, waved her into a chair good-naturedly. 'You've been out gallivanting again,' he said, doing a passable impression of a barrister addressing the court. 'What have you been up to? I know you never do anything without a reason. So, why are you here? What do you want?'

'Can't a girl visit her own father without there having to be a reason?' she responded, sounding suitably indignant. 'Oh, come now, dear girl,' he said with a wave of the hand, 'we both know the answer to that one.'

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

questions.instruction: 31. What had made Emma finally decide which car to buy?
options: A. She had had a very exciting test drive in it.
B. She reacted against Vinny's reservations.
C. She trusted the opinion of an expert on cars.
D. She was impressed by its power.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Đoạn văn nói rõ: 'Vinny had inspected it and expressed qualms. Was it not a bit too powerful for a woman driver, he offered. That clinched it. She bought the car the next day.' — 'That clinched it' (điều đó quyết định) chỉ ra rằng chính sự phản đối mang tính phân biệt giới tính của Vinny đã thúc đẩy Emma mua xe. Emma phản ứng lại (reacted against) sự dè dặt/lo ngại (reservations) của Vinny. Bẫy: D hấp dẫn vì đề cập 'power' nhưng Emma không ấn tượng bởi sức mạnh — cô PHẢN ỨNG lại thái độ cho rằng phụ nữ không nên lái xe mạnh. A không có bằng chứng trong bài. C sai vì Vinny không phải chuyên gia xe. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference & attitude.
questions.answerLocation: That clinched it. She bought the car the next day.
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 32. What do we learn about Emma's visits to her father at work?
options: A. He was usually rude to her and she hated going.
B. She would never let him know in advance.
C. He was often too busy to see her.
D. They shared similar feelings about them.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Câu then chốt: 'Her father did not encourage the habit, nor was it something she particularly enjoyed herself.' — Cả hai đều không thích việc thăm viếng: bố không khuyến khích, Emma cũng không đặc biệt thích. Họ chia sẻ cảm giác tương tự (similar feelings) về việc này. Bẫy: B hấp dẫn vì hôm nay Emma 'call on him unannounced' nhưng đó chỉ là HÔM NAY, không phải LUÔN LUÔN — câu hỏi hỏi về thói quen chung. A sai vì bố không thô lỗ (waved her in good-naturedly). C không có bằng chứng trong bài. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail & shared opinion.
questions.answerLocation: Her father did not encourage the habit, nor was it something she particularly enjoyed herself
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 33. What do we learn about the photographs?
options: A. They bore a very strong likeness to him.
B. They proved that he was a very popular husband and father.
C. He probably used them to impress people.
D. They obstructed the view from his desk.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'Emma suspected that the display was more for the benefit of visitors than for the man sitting behind the desk.' — Emma nghi ngờ rằng việc trưng bày ảnh là để gây ấn tượng với khách đến thăm (visitors) hơn là cho bản thân ông. 'He probably used them to impress people' = ông có lẽ dùng chúng để gây ấn tượng. Bẫy: B hấp dẫn vì ảnh chụp gia đình nhưng Emma NGHI NGỜ mục đích thực sự — không phải bằng chứng ông là bố/chồng tốt. A không được đề cập. D không có cơ sở. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: the display was more for the benefit of visitors than for the man sitting behind the desk
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 34. How does Emma feel about her father's office?
options: A. Conditions there would not suit her.
B. She was disgusted because it was so dirty.
C. She secretly aspired to working in such a place.
D. It was not a fitting place for him to work.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu then chốt: 'She could not envisage working in such an environment' và văn phòng 'struck her as oppressive' (gây cảm giác ngột ngạt). Emma không thể tưởng tượng mình làm việc ở đó → điều kiện ở đó KHÔNG PHÙ HỢP với cô (would not suit her). Bẫy: C là NGƯỢC ý hoàn toàn — Emma KHÔNG hề muốn làm việc ở đó ('could not envisage'). B sai vì 'oppressive' là bầu không khí nặng nề, không phải bẩn. D sai vì Emma không nói nơi đó không phù hợp với BỐ, chỉ không phù hợp với MÌNH. Chuyên đề: Reading — attitude & opinion.
questions.answerLocation: She could not envisage working in such an environment
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 35. Emma's father
options: A. knew there was a purpose to her visit.
B. knew she was in trouble.
C. was going to defend her in court.
D. impersonated people to amuse her.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Bố Emma nói thẳng: 'I know you never do anything without a reason. So, why are you here? What do you want?' — Ông biết rằng con gái luôn có MỤC ĐÍCH khi làm bất cứ điều gì, bao gồm cả việc đến thăm. Và cuối cùng: 'we both know the answer to that one' — cả hai đều biết cô đến vì lý do cụ thể. Bẫy: D hấp dẫn vì ông 'doing a passable impression of a barrister' nhưng đó là miêu tả CÁCH NÓI của ông, không phải mục đích giải trí. B không có bằng chứng. C sai vì không ai đề cập tòa án theo nghĩa đen. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail & character understanding.
questions.answerLocation: I know you never do anything without a reason. So, why are you here?
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 36. The relationship between Emma and her father was
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Mối quan hệ thân thiện nhưng có khoảng cách được thể hiện qua nhiều chi tiết: bố vẫy tay 'good-naturedly' (thân thiện), gọi cô 'dear girl' (trìu mến), nhưng cô hiếm khi đến thăm ('Twice a year'), cả hai đều không thích việc thăm viếng, và việc đến thăm chỉ để 'maintain an illusion of father-daughter togetherness' (duy trì ảo tưởng về sự gắn kết). Bẫy: C ('warm and loving') quá thân mật — không có bằng chứng cho tình cảm nồng ấm thực sự, chỉ có 'illusion' (ảo tưởng). A và D quá tiêu cực — bố không thù địch mà khá vui vẻ. Chuyên đề: Reading — overall tone & inference.
questions.answerLocation: waved her into a chair good-naturedly... maintain an illusion of father-daughter togetherness
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about the UK video game industry. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the article. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Playing the Game

The UK games industry is now worth over £1 billion and is one of the most vibrant sectors of the creative economy. It employs thousands of highly skilled people in roles ranging from programming and art to marketing and quality assurance. Yet for all its commercial success, the industry remains surprisingly poorly understood by those seeking to enter it.

[Gap 37]

Today the industry is very commercial, very competitive and very much technology-driven. The cost of developing a top title for a next-generation console can easily exceed £5 million, and the financial risk involved means that publishers are increasingly reluctant to take chances on unproven talent.

[Gap 38]

Nick Wheelwright, head of development at a major UK studio, confirms this trend. 'We are looking for outstanding people with excellent academic records,' he says. 'The days when you could walk in off the street with a demo tape and land a job are well and truly over.'

[Gap 39]

The skills required extend well beyond pure programming. Publishers need people with expertise in marketing, business development and project management. A flair for communication and the ability to work under pressure are highly prized.

[Gap 40]

At the other end of the scale, games testers represent the most accessible entry point into the industry. Testing is often dismissed as unskilled work, but experienced testers develop an intimate knowledge of game mechanics and software behaviour that is genuinely valuable.

[Gap 41]

Rachel Wood is a senior lead artist at one of the UK's most respected studios. Her path into the industry was far from conventional and illustrates the range of backgrounds from which successful game developers can emerge.

[Gap 42]

Rachel emphasises the importance of understanding technology even in a creative role. 'You can be the most talented artist in the world,' she says, 'but if you can't work within the technical constraints of the hardware, your work will never make it into a game.'

[Gap 43]

Whatever route aspiring game developers choose to take, the message from the industry is clear: passion for games is necessary but not sufficient. Technical competence, creative flair and professional discipline are all essential ingredients for a successful career in this demanding but rewarding field.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph you do not need to use.

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: Perhaps inevitably, the development side of the industry has placed a greater emphasis on academic excellence. Recruiters now routinely demand first-class or upper second-class degrees in computer science, mathematics or physics, and competition for graduate positions at top studios is fierce.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: Now 29, she studied fine art at Plymouth University before drifting into game development almost by accident. 'I had no idea the industry even existed as a career option,' she recalls. 'A friend showed me a job advertisement and I thought I'd give it a try. That was seven years ago and I haven't looked back.'

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: Based near Leamington Spa, Codemasters employs more than 400 people and has an average employee age of just 23 or 24. The company recruits heavily from local universities and runs an internship programme designed to identify promising talent before graduation.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: For wannabe graphics artists, some degree of technological understanding is essential. But recruiters stress that traditional artistic talent remains the foundation. 'We can teach people the software,' says one recruitment manager, 'but we can't teach them to draw. A strong portfolio of life drawing, painting and sculpture will always impress us more than flashy computer graphics.'

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: Quite often you will have to play the same section of a game for many months on end, searching for bugs and documenting faults with meticulous precision. The pay, while not generous by industry standards, compares favourably with many graduate starting salaries, and the experience gained can serve as a springboard to more senior roles.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: To stand a chance of serious long-term success, publishers need to shift millions of units worldwide. With sales of premium titles reaching prices of up to £50 and development costs rising year on year, the financial model increasingly resembles that of the film industry, where a handful of blockbusters must subsidise a portfolio of less profitable releases.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: But the past few years have brought big changes to the way the industry operates. The cottage industry days, when a single bedroom programmer could create a best-selling game, are long gone. The scale of modern game production demands large, multidisciplinary teams working to exacting deadlines.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: SCi is one of the UK's longest-established games publishers and has seen these changes at first hand. 'Experience helps,' says a company spokesperson, 'but we also take on gap year students and recent graduates. What matters most is attitude and a willingness to learn.'

questions.instruction: Gap 37:
questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G nói về sự thay đổi lớn trong ngành ('the past few years have brought big changes') và rằng 'the cottage industry days... are long gone'. Nó nối liền mạch ý từ đoạn trước (ngành công nghiệp thành công nhưng ít được hiểu) sang đoạn sau (ngành bây giờ rất thương mại, chi phí £5 triệu). Từ nối: 'big changes' → 'Today the industry is very commercial' tạo sự chuyển tiếp tự nhiên từ quá khứ sang hiện tại. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — chronological transition.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 37 — after 'poorly understood' paragraph, before 'very commercial' paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 38:
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A nói về 'greater emphasis on academic excellence' — ngành yêu cầu bằng cấp cao hơn. Nó nối liền mạch ý từ đoạn trước (chi phí £5 triệu, nhà phát hành không muốn mạo hiểm với người mới) sang đoạn sau (Nick Wheelwright xác nhận: 'outstanding people with excellent academic records'). Từ khóa liên kết: 'academic excellence' (đoạn A) → 'excellent academic records' (đoạn sau). Chuyên đề: Text coherence — topic development.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 38 — after 'unproven talent' paragraph, before Nick Wheelwright paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 39:
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn C giới thiệu Codemasters (400+ nhân viên, tuổi trung bình 23-24, tuyển từ đại học). Nó nối tiếp đoạn về Nick Wheelwright (yêu cầu người có thành tích học thuật xuất sắc) bằng cách đưa ra ví dụ cụ thể về một công ty và cách tuyển dụng. Đoạn sau nói về 'skills required extend beyond pure programming' — Codemasters là cầu nối tự nhiên giữa yêu cầu học thuật và các kỹ năng đa dạng. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — exemplification.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 39 — after Nick Wheelwright paragraph, before 'skills beyond programming' paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 40:
questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn H giới thiệu SCi — nhà phát hành lâu đời, nói về kinh nghiệm và gap year students. Nó nối từ đoạn trước (marketing, business skills) sang đoạn sau (games testers — 'at the other end of the scale'). Cụm 'at the other end of the scale' trong đoạn sau tạo sự đối lập với vai trò cấp cao đã đề cập trong đoạn H. SCi cũng cung cấp ví dụ cụ thể về kinh nghiệm ngành. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — contrast & scale.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 40 — after 'marketing/communication skills' paragraph, before 'games testers' paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 41:
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E mô tả chi tiết công việc testing: 'play the same section for many months', 'searching for bugs', lương so với graduate salaries. Nó mở rộng thông tin về games testers từ đoạn trước (entry point, valuable knowledge) bằng cách mô tả thực tế công việc và mức lương. Cụm 'springboard to more senior roles' nối tiếp ý 'genuinely valuable experience'. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — elaboration.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 41 — after 'games testers' paragraph, before Rachel Wood paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 42:
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Đoạn B kể về Rachel (29 tuổi, học fine art ở Plymouth, vào ngành tình cờ). Nó cung cấp chi tiết về background của Rachel được giới thiệu ở đoạn trước ('far from conventional'). Cụm 'Now 29' bắt đầu tiểu sử, và 'studied fine art' giải thích tại sao con đường của cô 'far from conventional'. Đoạn sau Rachel nói về tầm quan trọng của technology → tạo sự đối lập thú vị với background fine art. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — biographical detail.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 42 — after Rachel Wood introduction, before Rachel's technology emphasis
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap 43:
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D nói về 'wannabe graphics artists' — cần hiểu technology nhưng tài năng nghệ thuật truyền thống vẫn là nền tảng ('traditional artistic talent remains the foundation'). Nó nối tiếp đoạn Rachel (technology quan trọng cho creative roles) bằng cách mở rộng lời khuyên cho aspiring artists nói chung. Cụm 'we can teach people the software but we can't teach them to draw' bổ sung cho lời Rachel về technical constraints. Đoạn kết luận sau đó tổng hợp các thông điệp. Chuyên đề: Text coherence — generalisation from specific example.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 43 — after Rachel's technology quote, before conclusion paragraph
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: You are going to read five texts about cosmetic surgery. For questions 44-53, choose from the sections (A-E). The sections may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
The Democratisation of Beauty

Section A

The desire to look younger and more attractive is hardly new. What is new is the extent to which science has placed the tools of physical transformation within reach of ordinary people. Cosmetic surgery, once the exclusive preserve of the wealthy and the vain, has become a mainstream consumer service. The notion that we might surgically arrest the ageing process, turning back the biological clock by a decade or more, exerts a powerful hold on the popular imagination. Clinics advertise their services with the same breezy confidence as high-street retailers, promising renewed youth and vitality with all the certainty of a money-back guarantee.

Section B

The social stigma that once attached to cosmetic procedures has largely evaporated. Celebrities discuss their treatments with disarming candour, and reality television programmes have normalised the idea that one's appearance is something to be engineered rather than accepted. In this new landscape, surgically enhanced beauty carries no less cultural currency than the natural variety. The democratisation of cosmetic surgery reflects a broader cultural shift: a growing impatience with the idea that physical attractiveness should be distributed by genetic lottery alone. People increasingly feel that if technology can level the playing field, it should.

Section C

Perhaps the single most transformative development in cosmetic surgery came not from within the field itself but from the pharmaceutical industry, with the introduction of botulinum toxin — Botox — as a cosmetic treatment. Botox democratised cosmetic enhancement by making it quick, relatively painless and affordable. The industry has also embraced a consumer-driven model in which patients — or clients, as many clinics prefer to call them — self-diagnose their aesthetic deficiencies and present themselves for treatment with a clear idea of what they want. The traditional medical model, in which the doctor identifies the problem and prescribes the solution, has been largely inverted.

Section D

For many patients, the psychological benefits of cosmetic surgery are undeniable. Research consistently shows that the majority of people who undergo procedures report increased self-confidence and greater satisfaction with their appearance. Yet these gains are almost invariably temporary. The ageing process does not stop simply because a surgeon has intervened, and many patients find themselves returning for further procedures in an escalating cycle of surgical maintenance. The pursuit of surgical perfection, like the pursuit of perfection in any domain, is ultimately a race that cannot be won.

Section E

Critics of the cosmetic surgery industry argue that its rapid expansion has outpaced meaningful ethical scrutiny. The commercialisation of appearance, they contend, imposes unrealistic beauty standards that benefit the few — principally the surgeons and the corporations that profit from insecurity — at the expense of the many. The reluctance to engage in serious public debate about the industry's social impact is itself revealing: it suggests a collective unwillingness to confront uncomfortable truths about the values that drive consumer culture. Until society is prepared to have that conversation honestly, the cosmetic surgery industry will continue to operate in a moral grey area.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 44-53, choose from the sections A-E. The sections may be chosen more than once.

questions.instruction: 44. the ultimately futile nature of cosmetic surgery
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D nói rõ: 'The pursuit of surgical perfection... is ultimately a race that cannot be won' (cuộc đua không thể thắng) và 'these gains are almost invariably temporary' (lợi ích hầu như luôn tạm thời). 'Futile' = vô ích/vô vọng, phù hợp hoàn toàn với ý tưởng 'a race that cannot be won'. Đoạn này mô tả vòng lặp bất tận: phẫu thuật → lão hóa tiếp → phẫu thuật lại. Chuyên đề: Reading — paraphrasing & matching abstract concepts.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: The pursuit of surgical perfection... is ultimately a race that cannot be won
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 45. the idea that cosmetic surgery feeds our desire to cheat time
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Section A nói: 'The notion that we might surgically arrest the ageing process, turning back the biological clock by a decade or more, exerts a powerful hold on the popular imagination.' 'Cheat time' = đánh lừa thời gian ≈ 'arrest the ageing process' (ngăn chặn quá trình lão hóa) + 'turning back the biological clock' (quay ngược đồng hồ sinh học). Đây là paraphrase cấp cao. Chuyên đề: Reading — paraphrasing metaphorical language.
questions.answerLocation: Section A: surgically arrest the ageing process, turning back the biological clock
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 46. the evaporation of disapproval for cosmetic surgery in society
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B nói: 'The social stigma that once attached to cosmetic procedures has largely evaporated.' 'Evaporation of disapproval' = sự biến mất của sự phản đối ≈ 'social stigma has largely evaporated' (sự kỳ thị xã hội đã phần lớn bay hơi). Lưu ý: câu hỏi dùng chính xác từ 'evaporation' xuất hiện trong đoạn văn dưới dạng 'evaporated'. Chuyên đề: Reading — lexical matching & social concepts.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: The social stigma... has largely evaporated
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 47. the fact that cosmetic surgery eagerly embraces self-diagnosis
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C nói: 'The industry has also embraced a consumer-driven model in which patients... self-diagnose their aesthetic deficiencies and present themselves for treatment with a clear idea of what they want.' 'Eagerly embraces self-diagnosis' = nhiệt tình chấp nhận tự chẩn đoán ≈ 'embraced a consumer-driven model' + 'self-diagnose their aesthetic deficiencies'. Mô hình y tế truyền thống đã bị 'inverted' (đảo ngược). Chuyên đề: Reading — industry model & paraphrasing.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: patients... self-diagnose their aesthetic deficiencies
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 48. cosmetic surgery's ability to increase people's contentment
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D nói: 'the majority of people who undergo procedures report increased self-confidence and greater satisfaction with their appearance.' 'Increase people's contentment' = tăng sự hài lòng ≈ 'increased self-confidence and greater satisfaction'. Lưu ý: mặc dù Section D cũng nói lợi ích này là tạm thời, nhưng câu hỏi chỉ hỏi về 'ability to increase' — và Section D xác nhận khả năng này tồn tại. Chuyên đề: Reading — distinguishing between ability and permanence.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: report increased self-confidence and greater satisfaction
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 49. the view that cosmetic surgery's greatest breakthrough came from outside the field
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C nói: 'Perhaps the single most transformative development in cosmetic surgery came not from within the field itself but from the pharmaceutical industry, with the introduction of botulinum toxin — Botox.' 'Greatest breakthrough from outside the field' = bước đột phá lớn nhất đến từ bên ngoài lĩnh vực ≈ 'most transformative development... came not from within the field itself but from the pharmaceutical industry'. Botox là sản phẩm dược phẩm, không phải phẫu thuật thẩm mỹ. Chuyên đề: Reading — paraphrasing 'breakthrough' & 'outside'.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: the single most transformative development... came not from within the field itself
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 50. the idea that 'bought' beauty is no less valid than natural beauty
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B nói: 'surgically enhanced beauty carries no less cultural currency than the natural variety.' 'Bought beauty no less valid than natural beauty' = vẻ đẹp 'mua được' không kém giá trị hơn vẻ đẹp tự nhiên ≈ 'surgically enhanced beauty carries no less cultural currency than the natural variety'. 'Cultural currency' = giá trị/uy tín văn hóa. Cả hai cách diễn đạt đều khẳng định sự bình đẳng giữa vẻ đẹp tự nhiên và nhân tạo. Chuyên đề: Reading — abstract paraphrasing.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: surgically enhanced beauty carries no less cultural currency than the natural variety
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 51. the notion that cosmetic surgery benefits the few at the cost of the many
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E nói: 'its rapid expansion... imposes unrealistic beauty standards that benefit the few — principally the surgeons and the corporations that profit from insecurity — at the expense of the many.' 'Benefits the few at the cost of the many' = có lợi cho số ít mà thiệt thòi cho số đông ≈ 'benefit the few... at the expense of the many'. Cụm 'at the cost of' và 'at the expense of' là đồng nghĩa. Chuyên đề: Reading — synonymous expressions.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: benefit the few... at the expense of the many
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 52. people's unwillingness to engage in proper debate about cosmetic surgery
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E nói: 'The reluctance to engage in serious public debate about the industry's social impact is itself revealing: it suggests a collective unwillingness to confront uncomfortable truths.' 'Unwillingness to engage in proper debate' = sự không sẵn lòng tham gia tranh luận đúng đắn ≈ 'reluctance to engage in serious public debate' + 'collective unwillingness to confront uncomfortable truths'. Lưu ý: cả 'unwillingness' và 'reluctance' đều xuất hiện trong đoạn E. Chuyên đề: Reading — matching attitudes & social commentary.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: The reluctance to engage in serious public debate... collective unwillingness
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 53. people's growing intolerance that beauty is an unequalising force
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B nói: 'a growing impatience with the idea that physical attractiveness should be distributed by genetic lottery alone. People increasingly feel that if technology can level the playing field, it should.' 'Growing intolerance that beauty is an unequalising force' = sự không chấp nhận ngày càng tăng rằng vẻ đẹp là yếu tố tạo bất bình đẳng ≈ 'growing impatience with the idea that physical attractiveness should be distributed by genetic lottery alone'. 'Unequalising force' = lực lượng tạo bất bình đẳng ≈ 'distributed by genetic lottery' (phân phối bởi xổ số di truyền). 'Growing intolerance' ≈ 'growing impatience'. Chuyên đề: Reading — paraphrasing abstract societal concepts.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: a growing impatience with the idea that physical attractiveness should be distributed by genetic lottery
questions.source: Practice Test 1, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3
